statement.markdown: Implement a function to check if an input string represents a valid stack-based expression. The expression should be a sequence of parentheses and brackets. Return true if the expression is valid; otherwise, return false.
statement.inputFormat: An input string representing a stack-based expression of parentheses and brackets.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the stack-based expression is valid.
statement.notes: A valid stack-based expression is defined as a sequence of parentheses and brackets where every opening parenthesis or bracket is matched with a closing one. The valid pairs are: ( and ), [ and ], { and }. The input string may contain parentheses or brackets that are not matched to each other.

examples.input: Example input (matching the stdin format that main wrapper expects)

testCases.input: First line count, second line elements (stdin match)
